Participant Information Sheet

Research title: Cognitive decision making under uncertainty
Lead researcher: Dr. Jonathan Redshaw (School of Psychology, The University of Queensland – ARC Future Fellow)
Other researchers:

Miss. Cate MacColl (School of Psychology, The University of Queensland – Research Assistant)

PIS version number V01

Thank you for your interest in participating in this research project. Please note the following restrictions on participation before continuing:

  • You must complete this study on a desktop or laptop computer with a keyboard and mouse/trackpad. The main task will NOT work on a phone or tablet.

  • You must be at least 18 years of age.

  • You must have normal or corrected-to-normal vision. It’s okay to participate if you usually wear glasses or contact lenses, but please wear them while completing the task.

If you meet the above criteria, then please read the following information about the project so that you can decide whether you would like to take part in this research. Please feel free to ask any questions you might have about your involvement in the project. You can also talk to someone you trust, like a family member or friend, before deciding if you want to participate.

Participation in this research is voluntary. If you do not wish to take part, you do not have to. If you decide to take part and later change your mind, you are free to stop at any time, and you would not need to give any explanation for your decision to stop participating.

If you decide to take part, you will be asked indicate you have read and understand the information in this Participant Information Sheet and given the opportunity to download it to keep. Your decision of whether you take part, or not to take part, or to take part and then withdraw, will not affect your relationship with The University of Queensland.

What is the purpose of this research?

The purpose of this study is to investigate how individuals make basic cognitive decisions under situations of uncertainty. If you choose to participate, the main task will involve making simple, repetitive decisions about coloured objects, without having access to all the relevant information about these objects. The aim is to understand how people appraise and integrate relevant information in the context of an incomplete information landscape. This project is organised by the University of Queensland’s School of Psychology.

What does participation involve?

If you agree to participate, the experiment is expected to take approximately 50 minutes. Participation will involve completing the tasks below, and responding as described. First, you will be asked some demographic questions (age, gender, and postcode) to help us understand who is taking part and whether you are eligible (per the conditions above), before being redirected to the main task.

Participation involves viewing a grid of squares which will gradually reveal coloured balls. You will be directed to make predictions about which object will be randomly selected by a computer algorithm. After each response, you will be asked to indicate how sure you are in your answer, but you will not be provided with feedback on the accuracy of your response. More detailed instructions and an opportunity to practice will be provided at the commencement of the task.

After completing this first version, the task will be adapted slightly to allow you to decide how much information you would like to accumulate before making your final decision.

In the second version of the task, you will receive points proportional to your performance in the task. The three participants with the highest score will receive a bonus payment of £35 via Prolific in addition to the base payment.

After completing this task, you will be given some short surveys about your own beliefs and experiences of Intellectual Humility, Big 5 Personality Traits (OCEAN), Life Orientation, Risk Propensity, Self Control, Impulsive Behaviour. You do not have to answer any survey questions that you do not wish to answer.

Upon completion of the session, you will be redirected to finalise your submission. A researcher will then review your submission and grant your payment of £11.67, as well as any bonus payments you may be eligible for (as described above) in a timely manner.

What are the possible benefits of taking part?

Participation will result in compensation of £11.67. Pre-screening will be conducted per the conditions described above, and ineligible participants will be compensated with £0.10.

In addition to this base payment, the three participants with the highest scores will receive a bonus payment of £35 via Prolific upon the conclusion and review of the study. This amount is in addition to the base payment described above.

More broadly, participation contributes to the development of a better understanding of how people make cognitive decisions under conditions of uncertainty. However, no specific benefits to you are expected because of participation, beyond those outlined above.

What are the possible risks and disadvantages of taking part?

The most likely risk is mildly unpleasant feelings associated with completion of the surveys, which cover topics including intellectual humility (e.g., the tendency to change one’s mind), personality, life orientation (positive or negative outlook), and various measures relating to risk taking. You do not have to answer any survey questions that you do not wish to answer, and can reach out to the researchers and/or your local support services for information and support.

There are no other foreseen risks of participating beyond those that you would experience in everyday life (e.g., fatigued eyes or boredom from staring at a monitor for extended periods).

What will happen to the information about me?

By clicking proceed below you consent to UQ collecting, using and (where applicable) disclosing your information as set out in this Participant Information Statement. Information obtained in connection with this project will be retained and disposed of in accordance with relevant University of Queensland policies and State and National regulations. For further information regarding the management of research data and information, you may refer to UQ’s Research Data Management Policy.

Any information obtained in connection with this research project that can identify you will remain confidential. Confidentiality will be maintained by linking your data to a participant ID number rather than to your name to prevent your identification. This participant ID number will be linked directly to your data, so that researchers can identify your data should you choose to withdraw at any time. Beyond this instance, your data will remain unidentified.

It is anticipated that the results of this research project will be published and/or presented in a variety of forums. In any publication and/or presentation, information will be provided in such a way that you cannot be identified, except with your express permission.

The collected data may be used again for secondary/future research or submitted to a data repository to support transparency and reproducibility in research. Identifiable data will not be used in this way without your permission. Situations where collected data may be used again are described below.

Any information obtained during the research project is subject to inspection (for the purpose of verifying the procedures and the data) by the relevant authorities and authorised representatives of The University of Queensland, or as required by law. Designated University individuals (data stewards or custodians) may also have access to the information for the purposes of ensuring long term retention and disposal. By clicking proceed below, you authorise release of, or access to, this confidential information to the relevant research personnel and authorities as noted in this information sheet.

Personal Information

The personal information collected for this research project includes current age, gender and postcode.

Collection – Your personal information is collected for the purpose describing and accounting for any differences in our sample

Use – Your personal information will be used for the purpose of the research project and any other purpose described in this Participant Information Sheet.

Storage and security – Your personal information will be maintained confidentially by ensuring appropriate access and security is applied to the research project data. Your personal information will be accessed by authorised research staff, authorised staff of UQ (for the purposes of managing data holdings) and third parties as described in this Participant Information Sheet.

Disclosure – Your personal information will only be disclosed in a manner consistent with this Participant Information Sheet. Your personal information will remain confidential and will not be disclosed to a third party without your agreement, or as required by law.

You have the right to request access to the personal information about you that is collected and stored by the research team. If you are seeking to update or correct any information, it is recommended that you liaise with the research team in the first instance (see contact details at the top of this information sheet).

You are also able to request amendment via a University of Queensland Right to Information and Privacy officer (rti@uq.edu.au). For further information regarding the management of your personal information, please refer to UQ’s Research Data Management Policy, Privacy Management Policy and Privacy Management Procedure.

What will happen if I decide to withdraw?

Your participation in this research is voluntary and you are free to withdraw without needing to provide an explanation. If you withdraw from this study, you will not receive any penalty or bias as a result of your withdrawal. If you decide to withdraw after beginning the experiment, you can do so at any point without consequence by simply exiting from the task.

If you decide to withdraw before completion, your data will not be saved, and you will not be compensated for participation up to that point.

If you wish to withdraw from the study and have your data removed at any point after completion, please contact the researcher via Prolific message or the listed email.

Can I hear about the results of this research?

Results will be submitted for publication in open access journals upon the completion of the project. If you wish to receive information about the outcome of this study, please email Cate MacColl at c.maccoll@uq.edu.au with your request.

Who can I contact if I have any concerns about the project?

Questions about the nature of this project and participation in it should be directed to the research team (see top of information sheet for details).

This study adheres to the principles of the National Statement on Ethical Conduct in Human Research. The study has received ethics approval in accordance with the review processes of The University of Queensland. You are free to discuss concerns regarding this study or your participation in it with the research team, but if you would like to speak to an officer of the University not involved in the study, you may contact the Human Research Ethics Team via email humanethics@research.uq.edu.au or phone (see contact details at: https://research-support.uq.edu.au/research-support/ethics-integrity-compliance/contacts).

By clicking proceed below, you declare the following:

I confirm I am at least 18 years of age, have normal (or corrected-to-normal) vision and will complete the study on a desktop or laptop computer with a keyboard and mouse/trackpad.

I have read this Participant Information Sheet and have downloaded a copy if desired.

I consent for my non-identifiable data to be used in future research that is an extension of or related to this project.

I consent for my non-identifiable data to be used in any future research, including sharing with external researchers.

I understand the purposes, procedures and risks of the research.

I have had an opportunity to ask questions and have received satisfactory answers via message or email.

I understand that I will not receive any benefit from my involvement in the project except as detailed in this Participant Information Sheet.

I freely agree to participate in this research project and understand that I am free to withdraw at any time. I understand what will happen to the data and information if I decide to withdraw.
